notes: Missed May 23-24 workouts are dropped, not moved forward. May 29 is blocked on Google Calendar, so the week's single hard bike stimulus shifts to Saturday at a reduced dose. The objective is to restart rhythm, protect consistency, and avoid catch-up fatigue.

workouts.title: Easy Aerobic Re-entry
workouts.objective: Restart rhythm after the missed weekend with low-stress aerobic work.
workouts.why_now: The weekend workouts were missed for life reasons, so this is not a make-up ride. It gives the legs one easy bike touch before gym and the later key session.

targets.notes: Outdoor if easy to fit. 45-60 minutes at 50-65% FTP. This should feel like rhythm, not training debt.

warmup.notes: Ramp naturally. Do not chase watts.

steps.notes: Light cadence pickup. Smooth, not hard.

cooldown.notes: Keep it conversational and finish fresh.

nutrition.pre: Normal meal timing is enough. If starting hungry, take 20-30g carbs 30-60 min before.
nutrition.during: Water to thirst. No ride carbs needed unless you start hungry.
nutrition.post: Normal meal with 25-35g protein; add 30-60g carbs if dinner is delayed.
nutrition.supplements: Today's workout needs: none required. Daily baseline only if already part of routine.

recovery.post_session: Normal meal, fluids to thirst, and no extra training.
recovery.evening: Protect sleep. The value today is rhythm, not fatigue.
recovery.next_morning_check: Gym is fine only if legs feel normal and the ride felt easy.

skill_focus.action: Use 3 x 2 min at 95-100 rpm only if power stays capped.
skill_focus.why: This wakes up coordination without adding intensity before the real key ride later in the week.

skill_focus.action: Keep hands and jaw relaxed during cadence changes.
skill_focus.why: Relaxed upper body supports smoother power and lower fatigue cost.

mental_focus.intention: Restart rhythm without paying back missed work.
mental_focus.why: Consistency improves faster when missed sessions are absorbed instead of crammed.
mental_focus.reset: If you start chasing speed, shift easier, exhale once, and ride below the cap for 2 minutes.

pre_interval_cues: Start only if the 45-60 minute window is clean.
If life is still messy, skip without debt.
execution_notes.success_criteria: You finish fresher than you started and the week feels back on track.

workouts.title: Full-Body Strength Maintenance
workouts.objective: Keep real full-body strength in the week while protecting Saturday's key ride.
workouts.why_now: The missed weekend reduces bike fatigue, but life stress still counts. This keeps strength useful without turning lower body into soreness.

targets.notes: 60 minutes. Lower body RPE 5-6 with 2-3 reps in reserve. Upper body RPE 6-7. No failure and no soreness chasing.

centr_recommendation.primary: Centr full-body or upper/lower strength session, using the coach table as the prescription
alternatives: Strength for Sports upper-body plus lower-body basics
centr_recommendation.notes: Use Centr for demos or substitutions; keep lower body controlled and upper body honest.

gym_table.exercise: Dumbbell bench press or machine chest press

pre_interval_cues: This is useful strength, not catch-up training.
during_workout_cues: Upper body can work harder than legs.
execution_notes.if_too_easy: Add one upper-body accessory set, not heavy leg volume.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: Reduce lower-body work to one set each.
execution_notes.success_criteria: No leg heaviness the next morning.

workouts.title: Easy Aerobic Check
workouts.objective: Add easy aerobic work and confirm that the week is settling after the missed weekend.
workouts.why_now: This keeps bike frequency without stacking intensity before the Saturday key ride.

targets.notes: Outdoor only. 50-68% FTP. This is easy frequency, not a hidden tempo ride.

nutrition.pre: Normal meal timing is enough. If hungry, add 20-30g carbs 30-60 min before.
nutrition.during: Water to thirst. No ride carbs needed.
nutrition.supplements: Today's workout needs: none required. Daily baseline only if already part of routine.

recovery.post_session: Normal meal and fluids to thirst.
recovery.next_morning_check: If legs feel heavier on Thursday, keep Thursday blank and protect Saturday.

during_workout_cues: Let the ride feel almost too easy.
execution_notes.success_criteria: Easy ride feels easy and leaves Saturday protected.

workouts.title: Controlled Over-Under Re-entry
workouts.objective: Build threshold control with one reduced-dose hard stimulus, not an FTP test.
workouts.why_now: Friday is calendar-blocked, so the key bike session moves to Saturday. Because the weekend was missed and life stress was high, this uses two controlled sets instead of the original three.

source_context.week_objective: Restart rhythm and touch threshold control without catch-up fatigue.

targets.notes: Outdoor only. Execute only if freshness is good. This is the week's single hard stimulus and not a make-up workout.

nutrition.pre: Carb-forward meal 2-4h before with about 1-2g carbs/kg. Optional 20-40g simple carbs 30-60 min before.
nutrition.during: 30-60g carbs/hour if the ride goes over 75 minutes; otherwise water is acceptable if well-fed.
nutrition.post: 25-35g protein plus 60-90g carbs, especially because Sunday may include endurance.
nutrition.supplements: Today's workout needs: optional caffeine only if tolerated and not late day, about 1-3 mg/kg 30-60 min before. Daily baseline only if already part of routine.

recovery.post_session: Carbs plus protein, fluids to thirst, and 5-10 min easy spin/walk if legs tighten.
recovery.evening: No extra hard training. Keep dinner digestible and protect sleep.
recovery.next_morning_check: Sunday long ride happens only if legs, mood, and family schedule are good.

skill_focus.action: Rebuild power over 5-8 pedal strokes after corners or interruptions.
skill_focus.why: This trains outdoor threshold control without wasting energy in spikes.

skill_focus.action: Keep cadence steady and avoid torque spikes, especially late in each set.
skill_focus.why: Smoother torque helps protect the left calf and makes higher FTP work repeatable.

mental_focus.why: Controlled threshold work raises sustainable power better than turning a disrupted week into a test.
mental_focus.reset: If breathing or cadence gets ragged, ride 90 seconds at the low end of the target; if it still feels forced, finish the set and skip remaining work.

pre_interval_cues: Skip or downgrade if the week still feels messy.
Two good sets beat three forced sets.
during_workout_cues: If cadence or breathing collapses, stop the set.
execution_notes.if_too_easy: Add only 10-15 minutes easy endurance. Do not add a third over-under set.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: Convert to 3x8 minutes at 88-92% or ride endurance only.
execution_notes.success_criteria: Controlled work with no desperation and enough freshness left for Sunday if schedule allows.

workouts.title: Long Aerobic Endurance Flexible Cap
workouts.objective: Build aerobic durability if Saturday is absorbed, with a clear cap to avoid make-up fatigue.
workouts.why_now: The missed long ride from May 24 is dropped. This ride is the normal endurance support for the adjusted week, not repayment for the weekend.

source_context.week_objective: Restart rhythm and keep aerobic durability moving.

targets.notes: Outdoor only. 58-72% FTP. Default 2h15-2h30. Extend to 2h45 only if Saturday was controlled and family timing is easy.

steps.notes: Aerobic endurance and fueling practice. Keep climbs controlled.

nutrition.pre: Carb-forward meal 2-4h before with about 1-2g carbs/kg.
nutrition.during: Start in the first 30 min. Target 40-70g carbs/hour depending on gut tolerance and duration.
nutrition.post: 25-35g protein plus 60-100g carbs; use a simple recovery meal if dinner is delayed.
nutrition.supplements: Today's workout needs: electrolytes/sodium if hot or heavy sweating; otherwise none required. Daily baseline only if already part of routine.

recovery.post_session: Carbs plus protein, fluids to thirst, and easy walking if legs tighten.
recovery.evening: No extra training. Protect sleep and Monday freshness.
recovery.next_morning_check: If legs are heavy or HRV/resting HR worsens, reduce Tuesday gym lower-body work.

skill_focus.action: Keep climbs capped and seated when practical.
skill_focus.why: This builds aerobic durability without turning endurance into threshold spikes.

skill_focus.action: Check shoulders, jaw, and grip every 20-30 minutes.
skill_focus.why: Relaxed upper body reduces wasted energy over longer rides.

mental_focus.intention: Build patient durability and fuel early.
mental_focus.why: Long aerobic consistency makes higher FTP usable outside, not only in short efforts.
mental_focus.reset: If you get impatient or chase speed, drink, eat if due, relax grip, and ride below the endurance cap for 2 minutes.

pre_interval_cues: Start only if Saturday was controlled and family timing is clean.
If Saturday was costly, ride 75-120 minutes easy or rest.
execution_notes.if_too_easy: Cap at 2h45. Do not make up the missed May 24 ride.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: Shorten to 75-120 minutes easy or stop.
execution_notes.success_criteria: Finish steady, fueled, and not depleted.
